---
title: "01 · Catalog pull"
subtitle: "Pigmentation loci from the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog"
---

## What this workbook produces

**Output A** — `output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv`: every pigmentation
association the GWAS Catalog files under a small, reviewed set of root traits (and
their child traits). Each pull is stamped with the UTC time it hit the catalog and a
dated copy is archived under `output/catalog/versions/`, so you can always go back to
exactly what a previous pull returned.

## How the pull works

The engine (`scripts/gwas_catalog.py`) is config-driven: `scripts/traits_pigmentation.json`
names a handful of ontology **root** traits (EFO/OBA/MONDO short forms) — skin
pigmentation, facial pigmentation, freckles, sunburn, suntan, sun sensitivity, melanin
measurement — and the catalog's *download* endpoint returns every locus filed under each
root **and its child traits** (`includeChildTraits=true`). That means a study added after
this list was authored is still captured, without hardcoding a gene list that would go
stale and hide its provenance.

Three fail-loud guarantees protect the pull: every root must return >0 rows; the config's
**anchor** SNPs (SLC24A5 `rs1426654`, HERC2 `rs12913832`) must survive or the run errors;
and any mapped trait not in the reviewed snapshot is flagged for review.

```{python}
import sys, json
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
from gwas_catalog import load_config, run_axis, write_cache, assert_anchors

# Cache-first: with a cache present this loads Output A with NO network call, so the
# workbook renders identically every time. Set REFRESH = True to pull fresh from the
# live catalog (which archives a new dated version under output/catalog/versions/).
REFRESH = False

cfg = load_config("scripts/traits_pigmentation.json")
loci, meta = run_axis(cfg, refresh=REFRESH)
if REFRESH:
    write_cache(loci, meta, cfg)

print(f"{len(loci)} lead SNPs — queried {meta.get('queried_utc')}")
```

## Anchor check

If either anchor is missing, the pull is not capturing the loci this axis is built on and
this cell raises (do not trust the output downstream).

```{python}
assert_anchors(cfg.axis, loci, cfg.anchors)
```

## Where the signal lives — genome-wide view

A Manhattan plot of every positioned locus: genomic position across the autosomes (x) against
statistical strength `-log10(p)` (y), colored by pigmentation trait. Hover any point for the
rsID, gene, position, trait, and exact p-value. The chr15 (HERC2/OCA2/SLC24A5) and chr16 (MC1R)
stacks that dominate pigmentation genetics are immediately visible; the fail-loud anchors sit
exactly where they should.

```{python}
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from vizhelpers import neglog10p, trait_category, genome_layout, TRAIT_COLORS

CLIP = 330  # display ceiling for -log10(p); HERC2 (~9237) etc. shown as ▲ pinned at the cap

g, ticks, ticklabels = genome_layout(loci, chr_col="chr", pos_col="pos_hg38")
g["mlogp"] = g["pvalue"].map(neglog10p)
g = g.dropna(subset=["mlogp"]).copy()
g["cat"] = g["mapped_trait"].map(trait_category)
g["clipped"] = g["mlogp"] > CLIP
g["y"] = g["mlogp"].clip(upper=CLIP)

fig = go.Figure()

# faint alternating chromosome bands so the eye can find each chromosome
for i, (c, sub) in enumerate(g.groupby("_chr", observed=True)):
    if i % 2 and len(sub):
        fig.add_vrect(x0=sub["_x"].min(), x1=sub["_x"].max(),
                      fillcolor="#000", opacity=0.035, line_width=0, layer="below")

# one trace per trait category (drives the legend), points colored by trait
for cat, color in TRAIT_COLORS.items():
    sub = g[g["cat"] == cat]
    if not len(sub):
        continue
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=sub["_x"], y=sub["y"], mode="markers", name=cat,
        marker=dict(color=color, size=6, opacity=0.6,
                    symbol=["triangle-up" if c else "circle" for c in sub["clipped"]]),
        customdata=sub[["rsid", "mapped_gene", "chr", "pos_hg38", "mapped_trait", "pvalue"]].values,
        hovertemplate=("<b>%{customdata[0]}</b> · %{customdata[1]}"
                       "<br>chr%{customdata[2]}:%{customdata[3]}"
                       "<br>%{customdata[4]}<br>p = %{customdata[5]}<extra></extra>"),
    ))

fig.add_hline(y=7.30, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888",
              annotation_text="genome-wide p = 5×10⁻⁸", annotation_position="top left")

# label the canonical pigmentation loci at their real coordinates
for rs, gene in {"rs12913832": "HERC2", "rs1426654": "SLC24A5", "rs1805007": "MC1R",
                 "rs16891982": "SLC45A2", "rs1042602": "TYR", "rs12203592": "IRF4"}.items():
    r = g[g["rsid"] == rs]
    if len(r):
        fig.add_annotation(x=r["_x"].iloc[0], y=r["y"].iloc[0], text=gene,
                           showarrow=True, arrowhead=0, arrowwidth=0.7, ax=0, ay=-16,
                           font=dict(size=9, color="#333"))

fig.update_layout(
    title=f"{len(g)} of {len(loci)} pigmentation loci across the genome (colored by trait)",
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=ticks, ticktext=ticklabels, title="chromosome", showgrid=False),
    yaxis=dict(title=f"−log10(p) · clipped at {CLIP} (▲)", zeroline=False),
    template="simple_white", height=470, legend_title_text="trait",
    margin=dict(t=56, l=64, r=16, b=48),
)
fig
```

*Loci lacking catalog coordinates (7) are not plotted. `or_beta` is deliberately not encoded —
this pull mixes odds ratios and betas (see workbook 2), so it would mislead as size or color.*

## Provenance (sidecar `meta.json`)

```{python}
print(json.dumps(meta, indent=2))
```
